strengths: 15+ years of fullstack experience with TypeScript, Node.js, React, and MongoDB in production (Optilyz, RIDE, re:cinq)
Current hands-on AI agent development: MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks at re:cinq
Strong testing culture: TDD practitioner, author of test libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), taught testing courses; plus Docker/Kubernetes and AWS experience
concerns: No Nest.js experience mentioned, and frontend history leans heavily on Ember.js over React
No Kotlin and only limited Java exposure (robotics work at Altom, ~2016-2018); no Python listed
Specified test frameworks (Jest, Vitest, Playwright) not explicitly named; Selenium-era tooling is dated

key_factors.factor: Depth and duration of fullstack TS/Node experience
Gaps in Nest.js/Kotlin and named test frameworks

response.justification: Against the core competencies: fullstack TS/Node/React is well evidenced (pricing platform at Optilyz with TypeScript, AWS CDK, React, MongoDB); AI agent development is directly evidenced by MCP code generators and agentic frameworks at re:cinq — a rare, explicitly required skill; databases (MongoDB, MySQL, SQL), cloud/containerization (AWS CDK, Docker, Kubernetes), and Git are all present; testing expertise is evidenced by TDD practice, authored test libraries, and testing workshops, though Jest/Vitest/Playwright are not named; craftsmanship and communication are supported by talks, workshops, and code kata facilitation plus a CS degree. Absent evidence: Nest.js, Kotlin, and Python, with React depth partially offset by heavier Ember usage. The strong match on the hardest requirements (AI agents, architecture breadth, testing rigor) outweighs framework-level gaps that a 15-year polyglot can close quickly.
